title: Contextual Priming
concept: Loading relevant memories into context at task startup to prime the agent with past successes or failures.

exercises.prompt: What is one way to quickly find a specific memory from a big collection of memories?
exercises.explain: Key-value indexing allows for fast and precise retrieval of specific memories based on defined keys.

cards.title: What is Contextual Priming?
cards.body: Contextual priming helps an agent recall relevant memories for a task. It prepares the agent to use past experiences effectively.

cards.title: Definition of Contextual Priming
cards.body: Contextual priming is the process of activating related memories when starting a task. This means pulling in useful past experiences to help with the present challenge.

cards.title: Analogy for Contextual Priming
cards.body: Think of contextual priming like opening a drawer with specific tools before starting a project. You grab just the tools you need from previous projects to make your work easier.

cards.title: Example of Contextual Priming
cards.body: Before solving a math problem, a student remembers techniques from similar problems they solved in the past. This recall primes them for success.

cards.title: Myth: Memories are Always Random
cards.body: Some believe memories pop up randomly. In truth, contextual priming helps bring relevant memories to the forefront when needed.

cards.title: Comparing Contexts
cards.body: In one context, memories are scattered; in another, they are primed for action.

left.text: Memories scattered like leaves in a storm.

right.text: Memories organized and ready for the task.

exercises.prompt: What helps an agent recall memories when starting a task?
exercises.explain: Contextual priming focuses on activating the right memories to support current tasks.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Contextual priming makes it more difficult to access relevant memories.
exercises.explain: False. Contextual priming makes accessing relevant memories easier during tasks.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank: Contextual priming activates related memories for ______.
exercises.sentence: Contextual priming activates related memories for tasks, helping an agent perform better.
exercises.explain: The correct term is 'tasks', as contextual priming specifically prepares agents for their current work.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following scenarios best illustrates contextual priming?

options.text: A chef remembers a dish while cooking a new recipe.
A person forgets their last vacation.
Someone randomly looks at old photos.

exercises.explain: The correct choice shows a chef using relevant past memory to aid in the current task of cooking.

exercises.prompt: How does contextual priming improve task performance in AI agents?

options.text: It randomly pulls memories without focus.
It prepares agents by activating related past experiences.
It disconnects the agent from previous knowledge.

exercises.explain: By activating related past experiences, contextual priming directly improves task performance, allowing agents to leverage their past successes.
